currentState: Still no new direct contact, but chapter6 turns the old exchange into a recurring signal. The relationship now lives as suspicion sharpened by repetition rather than by recognition.
powerDynamic: The original exchange belonged to Sylvanas in the moment, but the remembered trader now exerts pressure through pattern. An absent grain man is still shaping how she reads the road.

physicalDynamics.pattern: The trader pulls his wagon up and takes in the empty village while Sylvanas stands armed in the lane, letting the word plague do the work before she starts questioning him.
By chapter5 the relationship survives only as memory: patched coat, grain dust, broken nose, and the irritation of not having enough face left to hunt.
Chapter6 never puts him back on the page directly. Instead another grain man repeats the same farewell and makes Sylvanas go still because the old encounter suddenly has an echo.

nameUsage.sylvanasCallsTrader: Still framed by function — grain man, trader, old seller — but now with the weight of a repeated pattern behind it.
nameUsage.traderCallsSylvanas: Only remembered from the original exchange as someone who recognized Farstrider authority on sight.

history.event: The grain seller provides the party with the first practical road picture beyond Millhaven and confirms that Corin's Crossing is still functioning.
Sylvanas gives him one second look as he rides north, filing him as memorable without yet treating him as a known threat.
Chapter5 upgrades the memory into route-linked suspicion once Sylvanas realizes he came north out of the same feeder-road world they are now tracing south.
Chapter6 adds the exact same farewell from another grain-road contact, confirming that the old seller's parting words belong to a recurring signal rather than to private verbal color.

trajectory: Useful local exchange -> retroactive suspicion -> recurring-signal suspicion.
